# Testing

## Running Tests

```bash
go test ./... -count=1
```

The `-count=1` flag disables test caching to ensure tests always run against the current state.

## Test Files

| File | Package | What it tests |
|------|---------|---------------|
| `cmd/screwsbox/main_test.go` | `main` | CLI flags, env var parsing, `run()` wiring |
| `internal/model/model_test.go` | `model` | `LabelFor()`, `Dedup()`, helper functions |
| `internal/store/store_test.go` | `store` | SQLite CRUD: items, tags, containers, shelf resize, auth, OIDC config, search |
| `internal/store/export_test.go` | `store` | Export/import round-trip, data integrity |
| `internal/session/memory_test.go` | `session` | MemoryStore: create, get, delete, TTL expiry, cleanup, touch |
| `internal/session/redis_test.go` | `session` | RedisStore with real Redis (skipped if unavailable) |
| `internal/session/manager_test.go` | `session` | Manager: cookie handling, CSRF tokens, session lifecycle |
| `internal/server/handler_test.go` | `server` | HTTP handler tests via `httptest`: API endpoints, auth flows, grid rendering |
| `internal/server/handler_session_test.go` | `server` | Session management handlers: list, revoke, revoke-all |
| `internal/oidc/cookie_test.go` | `oidc` | Encrypted state/nonce cookie round-trip |
| `internal/oidc/provider_test.go` | `oidc` | OIDC provider creation, token claims extraction |

## Coverage

Current coverage by package:

| Package | Coverage |
|---------|----------|
| `internal/model` | 100% |
| `internal/store` | ~80% |
| `internal/server` | ~79% |
| `internal/session` | ~57% |
| `internal/oidc` | ~47% |
| `cmd/screwsbox` | ~38% |

Run with coverage report:

```bash
go test ./... -count=1 -cover
```

Generate an HTML coverage report:

```bash
go test ./... -count=1 -coverprofile=coverage.out
go tool cover -html=coverage.out -o coverage.html
```

## Test Approach

### Store Tests

Store tests use real SQLite databases created in temporary directories (`t.TempDir()`). Each test gets an isolated database -- no mocks. This catches schema issues and SQL bugs that mock-based tests would miss.

### Server Handler Tests

Handler tests use `httptest.NewServer` or `httptest.NewRecorder` with the full chi router. They exercise the middleware stack (auth, CSRF, rate limiting) and validate JSON responses.

### Session Tests

- **MemoryStore**: Tests TTL expiry, background cleanup goroutine, concurrent access
- **RedisStore**: Tests against a real Redis instance. Tests are skipped with `t.Skip()` if Redis is not available, so they do not fail in environments without Redis.
- **Manager**: Tests cookie read/write, CSRF token generation and validation, session creation with different auth methods

### OIDC Tests

Tests cover encrypted cookie round-trips (state/nonce) and claim extraction from ID tokens.

## Test Libraries

Tests use [testify](https://github.com/stretchr/testify) `assert` and `require` packages for assertions. No other test libraries are used.

## CI Integration

Tests run in two places:
- **Pre-commit hook**: `go test ./...` runs on every commit locally
- **GitHub Actions CI**: `go test ./... -count=1` runs on pull requests to `master`
